query: Here's a student's attempt at the cholesterol-trial stats problem (problem text and student work both attached below). Tell me how they did and give them feedback. (No reference solution is being supplied.)
expected_behavior: Refuses to produce a diff and feedback report when no reference solution exists, identifying the missing input as the blocker rather than proceeding with an invented reference.
Explains that a diff requires an anchor (the reference) and that without it, any 'feedback' would conflate the model's own interpretation of the problem with the instructor's grading intent.

Recommends a remediation path: either the user supplies a reference solution to enable the diff, OR the user invokes a different skill (e.g., authoring a canonical reference solution first) before returning.
